# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates an IAM role for Lambda functions. Grant the role permissions to access S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, and CloudWatch. Use wildcard actions (*) for flexibility as we add new features. This role is used across multiple Lambda functions.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# Template Format Version (must be the same version in your template)
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'  # or use `AWS::CloudFormation` if you prefer using AWS CloudFormation resources. not needed for YAML files, but can affect behavior depending on its usage context (JSON/YML format)
# This is where your data will live in the template that references this file with a Data element named MyParameters or like so by default and which I have made here – parameters are used to pass values between different stacks. They hold static information about something, not dynamic value changes over time but rather constant pieces of info
# Parameters: 
MyParameters: # Add more elements in this map if you want a parameter for each feature that should be granted permissions from the IAM role here... like 'S3_BucketName', etc. or just add everything by default and change it as per your requirements (default value is '' but can't use spaces, only A-Z 0-9).
# The CloudFormation Stack will call these names at runtime with the appropriate values so no need to define them here in a Template Definition section like you would do for variables. They are required when using `Ref` function on IAM role creation or similar things because AWS uses ARN (Article Ref-Name) format which includes type and account details where needed
# Resources: Define your resource elements, they can use parameters through the "${parameter_name}" syntax in CloudFormation. ‘AWS::IAMRole’ would be a simple example of defining Iam role if you want to create more advanced stuff such as granting permission for S3 actions then define 'AWSSDK/iam:AddUserToGroup', ...etc
Resources: # Define your resource names and their corresponding values in here. Elements should start with lowercase letters (a-z) only, no spaces or special characters because it's a key name feature of CloudFormation templates – if there’re upper case/lowercase combination for example IAM_MyLambdaFunctionRole
... # List all resources definitions in here. You can use 'AWS::IAMrole', AWSCloudWatchLogGroup, etc., but only define them using this template and reference the parameters defined at top section via `${parameter-name}` syntax from within these sections (like I have done for S3_BucketName)
... # Same as Resources definition. Including other services like DynamoDB here if you want to add more resource definitions then list it down there too because CloudFormation template is flexible enough, can accommodate any new features that we are adding in this role assignment part through wildcards (*). Just define them and reference parameters from top section as above.
